example-project

A small, focused command-line utility that does one thing well: it converts a directory of YAML files into a single normalised JSON document, deduplicating keys and reporting conflicts. It is designed to be embedded in build pipelines where stability and predictable output matter more than fancy features.

Table of contents

  • Installation
  • Quick start
  • Features
  • Roadmap
  • Contributing
  • License

Installation

The recommended way to install example-project is from PyPI. It is a pure-Python package with no compiled dependencies and supports Python 3.11 and newer on Linux, macOS, and Windows.

pip install example-project
example-project --version

If you want the bleeding-edge development version, install directly from the main branch:

pip install git+https://github.com/example/example-project.git@main

Quick start

Point the tool at a directory of YAML files. By default it writes the merged JSON to stdout, which makes it easy to compose with other Unix tools.

example-project ./configs > merged.json
jq '.database.host' merged.json

Features

  • Deterministic key ordering across runs — diffs are stable.
  • Explicit conflict reporting when two files define the same key with different values.
  • Schema validation against a JSON Schema document, if one is provided.
  • Streaming mode for directories too large to fit in memory.

Roadmap

Planned work for the next minor release:

  • [x] Add TOML input support alongside YAML.
  • [x] Publish prebuilt wheels for musl Linux.
  • [ ] Add a --watch flag for development workflows.
  • [ ] Native binary builds via PyOxidizer.

Contributing

Contributions are welcome. Please read CONTRIBUTING.md before opening a pull request. We try to respond to issues within a week and to pull requests within two weeks.

The development setup is straightforward:

git clone https://github.com/example/example-project.git
cd example-project
pip install -e ".[dev]"
pytest

License

Released under the MIT license. See LICENSE for full text.
